title: About your stay
historyParagraph: The name Sinodium originates from an ancient Roman settlement that once existed in this region. Historical sources mention that the settlement of Sinodium was destroyed during Roman military campaigns led by Octavian in the 1st century BC, during conflicts with the Illyrian tribe of the Delmatae. This historical reference highlights the deep cultural and historical roots of the area and connects the modern location to its Roman-era heritage.
paragraphs: Villa Sinodium offers comfortable self-catering accommodation in a quiet countryside setting, within easy reach of the Adriatic coast and nearby towns. The property is surrounded by open landscape and typical Dalmatian scenery, making it a calm place to unwind after a day at the beach or out exploring.

The layout works well for couples and families who want space to relax together, with practical amenities for cooking, dining, and time outdoors—including a private pool and terrace for sunny afternoons and evenings.

From here you can plan straightforward day trips to Krka National Park, the historic city of Šibenik, the Zrmanja River and its canyon scenery, and the vibrant waterfront city of Split, then return to a relaxed, rural pace in the evenings.
Whether you are focused on the sea, nature walks, or simply enjoying the villa, Villa Sinodium is intended as a welcoming, low-key base for a restorative stay in central Dalmatia.
